name: USA — California tenancy & employment (illustrative subset)
disclaimer: Illustrative rules for a demo. Not legal advice. Verify against current law and consult legal aid.

rules.citation: California Civil Code §1950.5 — security deposits are refundable; non-refundable deposits are not allowed
rules.plain_reason: In California, residential security deposits must be refundable; a landlord cannot label them non-refundable.

rules.citation: California Civil Code §1950.5 — deposit cap (commonly up to ~1-2 months' rent)
rules.plain_reason: California caps security deposits; demanding several months' rent as a deposit is excessive.

rules.citation: California Civil Code §789.3 — self-help eviction (lockouts, utility shutoff) is illegal
rules.plain_reason: A landlord cannot lock you out, shut off utilities, or evict without a proper court process and notice.

rules.citation: California Labor Code §510 / §1194 — non-exempt employees cannot waive overtime
rules.plain_reason: Eligible workers are owed overtime; a clause that makes you 'waive' it is not enforceable.

keywords: shall not work for a competitor
rules.citation: California Business & Professions Code §16600 — non-compete clauses are generally void
rules.plain_reason: In California, clauses stopping you from working for a competitor are generally unenforceable.

keywords: may change these terms at any time
rules.citation: Cal. consumer law — one-sided unilateral modification terms may be unconscionable
rules.plain_reason: A term letting one side change the rules anytime without notice is one-sided and may be unenforceable.
